steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses 'although' to grant that income constrained fuel choice while immediately noting other circumstances also mattered. The question asks what rhetorical function this concession serves.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'raises a preemptive objection': a preemptive objection anticipates a counterargument; this sentence instead qualifies an existing claim. ✗
  • Choice B — 'claims the model has no support': the text grants income's importance rather than dismissing it entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — 'acknowledges income's role while showing the model overstated it': the 'although' concedes income mattered but pivots to show other factors were also at play. ✓
  • Choice D — 'refutes the model': complicating a model is not the same as refuting it; the text does the former. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
